title: QA: detect_language_server and detect_language_id duplicate extension table (DRY)
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
dry
## Location
src/tools/lsp.rs:57-69 and src/tools/lsp.rs:340-356
## Violation
Two separate functions encode the same extension→language mapping. Adding a new language requires editing both — and the mappings are subtly different (e.g., `cc`/`cxx` exist in `detect_language_id` for C++ but only `c`/`cpp`/`h`/`hpp` exist in `detect_language_server`, meaning a `.cc` file gets language id "cpp" but produces "no language server known").

Quoted (lsp.rs:57-69):
```
fn detect_language_server(file_path: &str) -> Option<(&'static str, Vec<&'static str>)> {
    let ext = file_path.rsplit('.').next().unwrap_or("");
    match ext {
        "rs" => Some(("rust-analyzer", vec![])),
        "ts" | "tsx" | "js" | "jsx" => Some(("typescript-language-server", vec!["--stdio"])),
        "py" => Some(("pylsp", vec![])),
        "go" => Some(("gopls", vec!["serve"])),
        "c" | "cpp" | "h" | "hpp" => Some(("clangd", vec![])),
```

Quoted (lsp.rs:340-356):
```
fn detect_language_id(file_path: &str) -> &str {
    let ext = file_path.rsplit('.').next().unwrap_or("");
    match ext {
        "rs" => "rust",
...
        "cpp" | "cc" | "cxx" | "h" | "hpp" => "cpp",
```
## Mandated refactor
1. Introduce a single `const LANG_TABLE: &[(&str, LangSpec)]` where `LangSpec` carries `server_cmd`, `server_args`, `language_id`, and the set of extensions.
2. Derive both helpers by lookup into that table.
3. Add a test that asserts every extension in `detect_language_id` is also present in `detect_language_server` (or explicitly opted-out).
## Filed from QA review dispatch
